fault_hypothesis: Lines 70-71 contain a spurious post-processing step that pops the last element when both lists share the same last value, incorrectly removing a valid duplicate from the merged result.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition on line 70 should never be true for a correct merge; when both lists share the same last element the merged list should still contain both copies.
After the pop branch the merged list length must still equal the sum of both input lengths, which it cannot if an element was wrongly removed.
Immediately before returning, the merged list must contain exactly as many elements as both inputs combined.
The returned merged list must have length equal to the sum of the two input list lengths.
